question.content: Build the AC equivalent model stage by stage and derive Au, Ri, and Ro, then include the R4-short output conclusion. Output exactly one ASCII line with four semicolon-separated fields in the form Au:...;Ri:...;Ro:...;if_R4_short_uo:...; do not use '=' or any equation form, no Chinese characters, no prose sentence, and no extra text. Answer format: Au:...;Ri:...;Ro:...;if_R4_short_uo:... in that order; write only the final results, with no units, explanatory text, or equals signs.

step_1.content: Port-reduction / test-source method: collapse the right follower into an input port and an output factor first, then let the left cascode drive that reduced port.
step_1.key_point: This route works from the output side backward instead of reading the gain chain panel by panel.

step_3.content: Reduce the right transistor stage as an emitter follower loaded by R5||RL. Its base port therefore presents Rin,f=rbe3+(1+β3)*(R5||RL), and its local voltage factor is Af=(1+β3)*(R5||RL)/(rbe3+(1+β3)*(R5||RL)).
step_3.key_point: Once this follower is replaced by Rin,f and Af, the rest of the circuit only needs to deliver the base-3 signal.

step_5.content: With C3 bypassing the emitter resistor of T1, the left pair acts as a cascode current-delivery block: ui=i_b1*rbe1 and the collector-side current is approximately β1*i_b1. That current feeds the parallel load R4||Rin,f, so the base-3 voltage gain is Aport=β1*(R4||Rin,f)/rbe1.
step_5.key_point: The overall voltage gain is obtained by multiplying the port gain Aport with the follower factor Af.

step_7.content: The input resistance is read directly at the AC input node as Ri=R2||R3||rbe1. For the output resistance, ground the input, remove RL, and apply a test source at the output emitter node: the follower reflects the base-side resistance into the emitter as (rbe3+R4)/(1+β3), which is then in parallel with R5.
step_7.key_point: If R4 is shorted, the base of the output transistor is fixed at AC ground, so the follower receives no signal and uo becomes zero.

step_9.content: Combine the reduced-port gain, the follower factor, the input shunt, and the test-source output result, then keep the shorted-R4 conclusion unchanged.
step_9.key_point: This gives the same final Au, Ri, Ro, and if_R4_short_uo entries as the reference answer.
